content.problemStatement:
### Welcome to E-Commerce Order Management System 🛒
___

This hands-on SQL project will guide you through building and managing a real-world E-Commerce Order Management System using the Advanced SQL concepts that you have learnt.

___
Imagine you are a database administrator for an online retail platform. Your task is to design and optimize a relational database that efficiently tracks customers, orders, products, payments, and shipping details.

**What You Will Learn & Implement:**

✅ Joins & Relationships – Retrieve complex order and product data using different JOINS \
✅ Subqueries & Set Operations – Filter and merge critical data using Subqueries, UNION, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT \
✅ Aggregations & Analytical Functions – Analyze business performance with COUNT(), SUM(), RANK(), LEAD(), and LAG() \
✅ Using PARTITION BY – Perform advanced analytics by segmenting sales data per region \
and more. 

By the end of this project, you will have a fully functional E-Commerce Order Management System that mimics real-world retail operations. You'll also strengthen your problem-solving skills by debugging and optimizing queries.

#### Let's get started and build something amazing!
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
